steps.title: Spot the cause-and-effect structure
steps.content:
The first sentence establishes a prolonged period of Arabic-speaking rule in the region.

The second sentence describes what resulted from this: Arabic words entering Spanish.

The signal is that one sentence causes the next—we need a transition that shows an outcome following a setup.

steps.title: Apply the causal signal
steps.content: 'Consequently' signals that what follows is the result of what came before. The other choices would suggest illustration, contrast, or detail-narrowing—none of which fit. That matches choice D — D is correct.
